players.name: Lionel Messi
players.highlight: Captained Argentina to the 2022 World Cup title; chasing back-to-back trophies on home-continent soil.
players.bio: Widely regarded as one of the greatest players ever, Messi enters 2026 as Argentina's talisman and the tournament's biggest storyline.

players.name: Kylian Mbappé
players.highlight: Scored a hat-trick in the 2022 World Cup final; world champion in 2018 at age 19.
players.bio: France's captain and the most feared forward in the world, capable of deciding any knockout tie on his own.

players.name: Jude Bellingham
players.highlight: Star of England's Euro 2024 run with iconic clutch goals.
players.bio: The complete modern midfielder, Bellingham drives England's hopes of finally winning a World Cup.

players.name: Harry Kane
players.highlight: England's all-time top scorer and 2018 World Cup Golden Boot winner.
players.bio: A relentless goalscorer who also creates for teammates by dropping deep — England's reference point in attack.

players.name: Vinícius Júnior
players.highlight: Multiple Champions League titles with decisive goals in finals.
players.bio: Brazil's electric winger carries the seleção's hopes of a record-extending sixth World Cup.

players.name: Lamine Yamal
players.highlight: Became the youngest-ever Euro winner with Spain at Euro 2024.
players.bio: The teenage phenomenon of world football — Spain's reigning European champions are built around his right wing.

players.name: Jamal Musiala
players.highlight: Germany's standout creator since his teens, a constant threat between the lines.
players.bio: A silky attacking midfielder who glides past defenders and unlocks packed defenses for Germany.

players.name: Cristiano Ronaldo
players.highlight: All-time leading men's international goalscorer, playing a record sixth World Cup.
players.bio: At 41, Ronaldo headlines what is almost certainly his final World Cup — one last shot at the trophy that has eluded him.

players.name: Erling Haaland
players.highlight: Fired Norway to their first World Cup since 1998 with a record-breaking qualifying campaign.
players.bio: The most prolific striker of his generation finally gets a World Cup stage with Norway.

players.name: Mohamed Salah
players.highlight: Premier League legend who led Egypt back to the World Cup.
players.bio: Egypt's king and captain, carrying an entire nation's expectations into the tournament.

players.name: Santiago Giménez
players.highlight: Leads the line for co-host Mexico in front of home crowds at the Estadio Azteca.
players.bio: Mexico's modern No. 9 — the host nation's best hope of a deep home World Cup run.

players.name: Son Heung-min
players.highlight: Asia's biggest star, captaining South Korea at his fourth World Cup.
players.bio: Now playing his club football in Los Angeles, Son leads South Korea on familiar North American soil.
